definitions.gloss_en: terrain that is elevated above the surrounding area, granting advantages to units occupying it
definitions.gloss_jp: {周|まわ}りより{高|たか}い{場|ば}{所|しょ}にある{地|ち}{形|けい}；{占|せん}{領|りょう}した{ユニット|ゆにっと}に{優|ゆう}{位|い}を{与|あた}える
definitions.explanation_en: A terrain type at higher elevation. Units on high ground often gain shooting range advantages, line-of-sight advantages, or combat modifiers.
definitions.implicit_notes: 高地 is a classical Japanese military geography term extensively documented in WWII-era military writing. The phrase '高地を制する' (control the high ground) is a classical military maxim.
definitions.orthography_notes: 高地: 高 (high/tall) + 地 (ground/land). Goo dictionary lists it as a standard word meaning 'elevated land'. JWC terrain documentation attests it. Not WH-specific.
definitions.game_setting_notes: Standard wargame terrain type. Units occupying 高地 gain advantages specified in the terrain rules. No WH-specific concern.

usage_jp: {高|こう}{地|ち}を{占|せん}{領|りょう}する (occupy the high ground); {高|こう}{地|ち}の{ユニット|ゆにっと}は+1の{射|しゃ}{撃|げき}ボーナスを{得|え}る (units on high ground gain +1 to shooting)
comparative_notes: No direct WH concern. Classical military term with broad applicability in wargame and historical contexts.

examples.japanese: {彼|かれ}らはその{高|こう}{地|ち}を{敵|てき}{軍|ぐん}に{明|あ}け{渡|わた}した。
examples.english: They abandoned the hill to enemy forces.

cross_references.note: 地形 — terrain; 高地 is an elevation-type terrain
